justification: All pass criteria met: only v2 content used (AC1+AC3, no v1 AC2); v2 domain rule uses absolute prohibition language ('cannot be disabled under any circumstances') with legal requirement citation; AC3 present with exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; contradictions_resolved section explicitly notes v2 supersedes v1; MJ's 403 enforcement aligned with v2 prohibition in architecture_guidance.

observations.text: Clean separation — v1 AC2 (transactional opt-out) completely absent from acceptance_criteria
contradictions_resolved section clearly documents the v1→v2 evolution and why AC2 was removed
Exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED appears in domain_rules, architecture_guidance, and acceptance_criteria consistently
Architecture guidance explicitly says 'Do NOT silently ignore the field — fail explicitly' which aligns with v2's absolute prohibition
Low confidence areas transparently flag assumptions about defaults and mixed-payload behavior rather than guessing
